steps.action: Joget (Jawi: جوڬيت) is a traditional Malay dance with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite.
steps.text: Joget (Jawi: جوڬيت) is a traditional Malay dance with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite.

steps.action: It was influenced by the Portuguese dance of Bra
steps.text: It was influenced by the Portuguese dance of Branyo which is believed to have been spread to Malacca during the spice trade.

steps.action: In Malacca, joget dance is better known as Chaku
steps.text: In Malacca, joget dance is better known as Chakunchak.

steps.action: The dance is one of the most popular folk dances
steps.text: The dance is one of the most popular folk dances in Malaysia that is normally performed by couples in cultural festivals, weddings and other social functions.

steps.action: The dance is of the Portuguese roots and is acco
steps.text: The dance is of the Portuguese roots and is accompanied by an ensemble consisting of a violin of Western world, a knobbed gong of Asia, a flute (optional), and at least two rebana or gendang of Maritime Southeast Asia.

steps.action: The tempo of Joget music is fairly quick, with a
steps.text: The tempo of Joget music is fairly quick, with a feeling of teasing and playing between the partners.

steps.action: The music emphasizes duple- and triple-beat divi
steps.text: The music emphasizes duple- and triple-beat division, both in alternation and simultaneously, and is sung in the northeast Malaysian style.

steps.action: One of the most popular types of Joget is called
steps.text: One of the most popular types of Joget is called "Joget Lambak" which is usually performed by a large crowd together at social functions.

steps.action: In Indonesia, the term 'joget' is usually applie
steps.text: In Indonesia, the term 'joget' is usually applied to any form of popular street dance, such as that to dangdut music.

steps.action: Joget, an open source workflow software built in
steps.text: Joget, an open source workflow software built in Malaysia, is named after this dance.

steps.action: Joget gained popularity among the Malay communit
steps.text: Joget gained popularity among the Malay community in Singapore after its introduction in 1942.

steps.action: Sri Lankan Kaffiringna music style and Joget has
steps.text: Sri Lankan Kaffiringna music style and Joget has some related melodic variations.

steps.action: Both styles have a Portuguese influence, accordi
steps.text: Both styles have a Portuguese influence, according to Sri Lankan musicologist Ruwin Dias.
